text.title: Terse-default tool output with verbosity opt-in
text.summary: Default to one-line-per-result summary; expose a `format` parameter so the agent opts into the full payload only when needed.

section.title: The output rule
section.p: Verbose tool outputs poison every subsequent prompt. The default shape should be the smallest representation that still lets the agent decide what to do next — usually a count plus IDs plus one summary line per result. Full payloads stay one parameter away.

rule.statement: Any tool whose worst-case output exceeds ~500 tokens MUST default to a compact shape (summary line, table of IDs, top-N) AND accept a `format: Literal["summary","full"]` (or equivalent `verbosity` enum) parameter so the agent can opt into raw payloads on demand.
rule.rationale: SkillReducer (arXiv 2603.29919) compressed tool descriptions and bodies by ~40% and observed a 2.8% absolute task-quality LIFT — verbose context distracts the model and inflates per-call cost across the rest of the loop. Compact-by-default plus opt-in verbose preserves correctness without the bloat.

example.note: Default mode burns ~30 tokens/hit; agent escalates to `format="full"` only after a summary points it at the row that matters.
Every call dumps full rows into context, even when the agent only needs timestamps to plan.

antipattern.description: Summary mode that drops the IDs the agent needs to escalate to full mode.
antipattern.reason: If the summary is not pointable, the agent has to re-search to get the same row. Always include the primary key in the terse shape.

antipattern.description: Implicit truncation — silently cutting output to N tokens without telling the agent.
antipattern.reason: Model thinks it saw all results. Always return an explicit `truncated: true` flag plus the count it would have returned.

section.reference: Reference search tool with summary/full modes.
SkillReducer — compression improves agent task quality.
Shamsul Arefin — MCP code execution: from confusion to clarity.
